nodes.name: 01 · Webhook · Mailgun Events
nodes.notes: POST /webhook/mailgun-events. NO header auth — Mailgun signs every request with HMAC-SHA256, verified in Node 02. Public URL required (Mailgun must reach it). For localhost dev, use ngrok or Cloudflare Tunnel.

nodes.name: 02 · Code · Verify HMAC & Parse
nodes.notes: Pure JS. Verifies HMAC-SHA256(timestamp+token) signature against Mailgun signing key. Parses event-data into a flat object with token, event, timestamp, reason. REPLACE SIGNING_KEY const before activating.

nodes.name: 04 · Switch · Event Type
nodes.notes: Routes by event-data.event. 5 named outputs + fallback for unhandled types (unsubscribed, accepted, rejected/by-mailgun, etc). Fallback flows to Respond 200 — we ack but don't act.

parameters.query:
UPDATE document_collection_log
SET delivered_at = COALESCE(delivered_at, $1::timestamptz),
    status = CASE WHEN status IN ('queued','sent') THEN 'delivered' ELSE status END,
    email_message_id = COALESCE(email_message_id, NULLIF($2,'null')),
    updated_at = now()
WHERE token = NULLIF($3, 'null')::uuid
RETURNING id, status;

nodes.name: 05a · Postgres · Mark Delivered
nodes.notes: COALESCE on delivered_at: first-write-wins (Mailgun retries duplicate events). Status CASE: only upgrade from queued/sent — never downgrade an opened/clicked row.

parameters.query:
UPDATE document_collection_log
SET opened_at = COALESCE(opened_at, $1::timestamptz),
    status = CASE WHEN status IN ('queued','sent','delivered') THEN 'opened' ELSE status END,
    updated_at = now()
WHERE token = NULLIF($2, 'null')::uuid
RETURNING id, status;

UPDATE document_collection_log
SET clicked_at = COALESCE(clicked_at, $1::timestamptz),
    status = CASE WHEN status IN ('queued','sent','delivered','opened') THEN 'clicked' ELSE status END,
    updated_at = now()
WHERE token = NULLIF($2, 'null')::uuid
RETURNING id, status;

UPDATE document_collection_log
SET rejected_at = COALESCE(rejected_at, $1::timestamptz),
    rejection_reason = COALESCE(rejection_reason, $2::text),
    status = 'bounced',
    updated_at = now()
WHERE token = NULLIF($3, 'null')::uuid
RETURNING id, subcontractor_id, email_to, rejection_reason;

nodes.name: 05d · Postgres · Mark Bounced
nodes.notes: Mailgun emits both permanent (hard bounce) and temporary (soft bounce/deferral) 'failed' events. severity field on the parsed JSON distinguishes them. Conservative choice: always mark bounced, store reason, let downstream cleanup logic decide whether to retry.

parameters.query:
UPDATE document_collection_log
SET rejected_at = COALESCE(rejected_at, $1::timestamptz),
    rejection_reason = 'spam_complaint',
    status = 'rejected',
    updated_at = now()
WHERE token = NULLIF($2, 'null')::uuid
RETURNING id, subcontractor_id, email_to;

nodes.name: 05e · Postgres · Mark Complained
nodes.notes: Spam complaint is a brand-reputation event. Always alert Slack. Consider auto-suppressing this email_to address from future sends — handled by separate compliance workflow.

nodes.name: 06 · Slack · Bounce/Complaint Alert
nodes.notes: Posts to #bfc-compliance-alerts via incoming webhook. Only fires from 05d (bounce) and 05e (complaint). Replace REPLACE_WITH_BFC_COMPLIANCE_ALERTS_WEBHOOK with the webhook URL from Slack app config.

parameters.responseBody: ={{ { ok: true, event: $('02 · Code · Verify HMAC & Parse').item.json.event, token: $('02 · Code · Verify HMAC & Parse').item.json.token } }}

nodes.name: 07 · Respond · 200 OK
nodes.notes: Required. Mailgun retries any event for 8 hours without a 2xx. If you remove this node, every event lands 5+ times and your log rows appear to flap.
